description: A character record in The Ptah Protocol. A person, creature, or entity that exists inside a specific world. The character is not the user — the character is the world's inhabitant.

main.description: A birth certificate crossed with a passport. Ptah speaks the name and the thing exists — the character exists because it was named and the naming is permanent.

creatorDID.description: Who created this character. This never changes — it is the provenance that travels.

worldReference.description: The AT URI of the world record this character belongs to.

roleReference.description: The AT URI of the Role record this character is an instance of. If present, this character is a specific performance of a shared role rather than a wholly original character.

description.description: Who this character is, in plain language.

properties.description: Flexible fields — species, age, role, affiliation, abilities. Open-ended so any kind of world can use the same record type.

originType.description: The intellectual property origin of this character.

sourceReference.description: If originType is publicDomain, the specific source.

controlType.description: How control of this character is governed. Solves the Ghost Character problem in public domain worlds.

authorshipRecord.description: Permanent, immutable link back to the creator. This never changes even if the character gets contributed to, built on, or rendered a thousand different ways.

canonicalStatus.description: The canonical standing of this character within its world.

characterProperties.description: Flexible key-value properties for any kind of world. All fields optional — worlds define what matters.

role.description: Narrative or social role within the world.
